engineIntelligence.h2: Fiat Ducato Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Fiat Ducato in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Fiat Ducato F1AE0481D engine - diesel 2.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR valve clogging - causes power loss and rough idle from 60k miles on commercial duty cycles.
DPF regeneration failure - soot buildup blocks filter on short-journey vehicles, triggering limp mode.
Turbo wastegate actuator failure - boost pressure loss and P2262 fault codes common after 80k miles.

cards.altText: Fiat Ducato F1CE0481D engine - diesel 3.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger bearing wear - oil starvation causes boost loss and metallic rattling from 90k miles.
Injector seal diesel leak - external fuel seepage around rocker cover, misfire codes on high-mileage units.
AdBlue SCR system faults - Euro 6 variants experience NOx sensor failures and dosing pump blockages.

cards.altText: Fiat Ducato F1AE0481C engine - diesel 2.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: Oil pump chain drive failure - catastrophic lubrication loss risk on early units before 2010 revision.
Timing chain tensioner wear - cold-start rattling and guide failure common from 70k miles.
Cylinder head gasket seepage - coolant/oil mixing on high-mileage commercial vehicles.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,600-£2,200 (supply only) Estimated from comparable F1AE units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Fiat Ducato Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Fiat Ducato across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.3L Diesel
entries.history: Euro 4 base 2.3L MultiJet; backbone of X250 Ducato fleet vans; robust but EGR/DPF prone on short journeys.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van L2H2, Panel Van L3H2, Chassis Cab 35, Crew Cab 35, Motorhome Base 35
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging causing power loss from 60k miles on commercial duty cycles
DPF regeneration failure on short-journey vehicles triggering limp mode
Turbo wastegate actuator failure with P2262 fault codes after 80k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for F1AE0481D Fiat Ducato engine replacement

entries.title: 2.3L Diesel
entries.history: Revised Euro 5 2.3L with improved EGR cooling; most common UK Ducato engine code across X250/X290 generations.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van L2H2, Panel Van L3H2, Panel Van L4H2, Chassis Cab 35/40, Crew Cab 35, Motorhome Base
commonFailures: DPF soot load accumulation requiring forced regeneration or replacement on urban fleets
Timing chain tensioner wear causing cold-start rattling from 70k miles
Injector seal diesel leaks around rocker cover on high-mileage commercial units
entries.cta: Get quotes for F1AE3481D Fiat Ducato engine replacement

entries.title: 2.3L Diesel Euro 6
entries.history: AdBlue-equipped Euro 6 2.3L for ULEZ compliance; essential for London and CAZ commercial operators.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van L2H2/L3H2 Euro 6, Chassis Cab 35 Euro 6, Motorhome Base Euro 6, Minibus Euro 6
commonFailures: AdBlue SCR dosing pump failure causing NOx emissions faults and limp mode
NOx sensor degradation triggering dashboard warnings and MoT emissions test failure
DPF/EGR combined system clogging on stop-start urban commercial use
entries.cta: Get quotes for F1AE3481E Fiat Ducato engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Euro 4 3.0L heavy-duty MultiJet for chassis cab, tipper and motorhome converters; reinforced internals for high payload.
compatibleVariants: Chassis Cab 35/40/45, Tipper 35/40, Dropside 35/40, Motorhome Heavy Duty Base, Minibus PSV
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing wear causing oil consumption and boost loss from 90k miles
Injector seal external diesel leaks requiring rocker cover gasket refresh
Cylinder head gasket seepage on high-mileage heavy-duty commercial applications
entries.cta: Get quotes for F1CE0481D Fiat Ducato engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel Euro 5
entries.history: Revised Euro 5 3.0L with improved emissions calibration; preferred for motorhome converters requiring AdBlue prep.
compatibleVariants: Chassis Cab 40/45 Euro 5, Tipper 40/45, Motorhome Heavy Duty Euro 5, Minibus Euro 5
commonFailures: Turbo actuator electronic failure causing intermittent boost loss and fault codes
EGR cooler blockage reducing performance on high-mileage heavy-duty use
Dual mass flywheel vibration requiring clutch kit replacement from 100k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for F1CE3481D Fiat Ducato engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel Euro 6
entries.history: AdBlue SCR Euro 6 3.0L for ULEZ/CAZ compliance; essential for urban commercial and motorhome operators.
compatibleVariants: Chassis Cab 35/40/45 Euro 6, Tipper Euro 6, Motorhome Heavy Duty Euro 6, Minibus PSV Euro 6
commonFailures: AdBlue pump and injector faults causing NOx system warnings and limp mode activation
SCR catalyst efficiency degradation triggering MoT emissions test failure
Combined DPF/EGR/AdBlue system complexity increasing maintenance costs on urban fleets

Injection pump wear causing hard starting and uneven idle on high-mileage units
Turbocharger oil seal leaks requiring turbo refurbishment from 80k miles
Glow plug circuit failures causing difficult cold starts in winter conditions

commonProblems.h2: Fiat Ducato Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Fiat Ducato's 2.3L and 3.0L MultiJet diesel engines dominate UK replacement demand, with EGR/DPF issues on short-journey vans and oil pump chain failures on early 2.3L units being most critical. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Fiat Ducato EGR Valve Clogging - Why 2.3L MultiJet Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Ducato Panel Van L2H2/L3H2, Chassis Cab 35 (2006-2024, F1AE0481D/F1AE3481D/F1AE3481E)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation valve and cooler accumulate carbon deposits from commercial stop-start driving, restricting airflow and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - EGR Valve Clean/Replace - Address clogging before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean or replace EGR valve and cooler; reset adaptation values
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early; recurrence likely on short-journey commercial use

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Delete + DPF Clean - Bypass system (off-road use only)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR blanking, software remap, DPF chemical clean; not MoT/legal for road use
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix but illegal for road use; voids warranty and insurance

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with EGR Upgrade - Address root cause
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage commercial Ducatos; prevents recurrence

problems.recommendation: On a Fiat Ducato worth £8,000-£15,000 with over 80k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR components offers better long-term value than repeated valve replacements. For lower-mileage vans, a specialist EGR clean may suffice if caught early.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt F1AE MultiJet engine prices for your Fiat Ducato

problems.h4: Fiat Ducato DPF Regeneration Failure - Blocked Filter Costs
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Ducato all variants with F1AE3481D/F1AE3481E/F1CE3481D/F1CE3481E (2011-2024)
problems.rootCause: Diesel particulate filter cannot complete active regeneration on short-journey commercial routes, causing soot accumulation, increased backpressure and limp mode activation.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Forced Regeneration + Additive - Attempt to clear blockage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic reset, forced regen cycle, DPF additive treatment
repairOptions.longevity: Only works if blockage is mild; temporary fix for urban fleets

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - DPF Removal & Clean/Replace - Physical intervention
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove DPF, chemical/ultrasonic clean or replace with reconditioned unit
repairOptions.longevity: Effective but costly; new DPFs expensive; recurrence possible

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with DPF-Ready Spec - Comprehensive solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with DPF-compatible calibration, bench tested, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage Ducatos; addresses multiple failure points at once

problems.recommendation: If your Ducato does mostly short urban trips, a rebuilt engine with DPF-ready calibration offers the most reliable long-term solution. For mixed-use vans, a specialist DPF clean may extend life if performed before complete blockage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Ducato DPF-compatible rebuilt engines

problems.group: Fiat Ducato Oil Pump Chain Drive Failure
problems.h4: Fiat Ducato Oil Pump Chain Drive Failure - Early 2.3L Catastrophic Risk
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Ducato early X250 Panel Van/Chassis Cab (2006-2010, F1AE0481C)
problems.rootCause: Original oil pump chain drive tensioner design wears prematurely, causing chain slap, lubrication pressure loss and potential total engine seizure without warning.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative - Oil Pump Chain Upgrade Kit - Fix before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides with revised components; requires engine partial strip
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if engine still healthy; not possible after seizure occurs

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Mandatory Chain Upgrade - Only safe option post-failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with mandatory oil pump chain upgrade, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only recommended solution for F1AE0481C engines; prevents repeat failure

problems.recommendation: If your early 2.3L Ducato (pre-2010) has over 60k miles, budget for a rebuilt engine with the mandatory oil pump chain upgrade. This is not a repair-you-can-delay failure - seizure means write-off.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt F1AE0481C engine prices with mandatory chain upgrade

problems.group: Fiat Ducato Turbocharger Wastegate Actuator Failure
problems.h4: Fiat Ducato Turbocharger Wastegate Actuator Failure - Boost Loss Diagnosis
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Ducato all MultiJet variants (2006-2024, F1AE/F1CE series)
problems.rootCause: Turbo wastegate actuator diaphragm or electronic control fails, causing incorrect boost pressure, P2262 fault codes and significant power loss under load.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Actuator Replacement Only - Address symptom
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator, calibrate boost pressure, reset fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; turbo internals may still be worn

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Reconditioning - Address turbo unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, recondition bearings/seals/actuator, bench test, refit
repairOptions.longevity: Good value if engine otherwise healthy; 12-month specialist warranty typical

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Turbo Upgrade - Comprehensive
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo spec, bench tested, full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage commercial Ducatos; addresses multiple wear points

problems.recommendation: For Ducatos over 100k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo offers better value than standalone turbo work. For lower-mileage vans, a specialist turbo recondition may suffice if the engine block is sound.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Ducato turbo-upgraded rebuilt engines

problems.group: Fiat Ducato AdBlue SCR System Faults
problems.h4: Fiat Ducato AdBlue SCR System Faults - Euro 6 Compliance Costs
problems.rootCause: AdBlue dosing pump, injector or NOx sensor fails due to crystallisation, contamination or electrical fault, triggering emissions warnings and limp mode to protect SCR catalyst.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - AdBlue System Clean/Reset - Address crystallisation
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Flush dosing lines, clean injector, reset adaptation values, refill AdBlue
repairOptions.longevity: Only works for mild crystallisation; recurrence likely on stop-start use

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Component Replacement - Fix failed part
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace dosing pump, injector or NOx sensor; software calibration required
repairOptions.longevity: Effective but costly; new AdBlue components expensive

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Euro 6 Compliance - Future-proof
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt Euro 6 engine with upgraded AdBlue components, ULEZ compliant, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for urban commercial Ducatos facing daily CAZ charges

problems.recommendation: For London or CAZ-operated Ducatos, a rebuilt Euro 6 engine with upgraded AdBlue components offers the most reliable path to compliance. Avoid cheap component swaps that may fail again quickly.
problems.cta: Compare Euro 6 Fiat Ducato engine prices with AdBlue upgrade

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Fiat Ducato
finalCta.paragraph: EGR clogging, DPF blockage, oil pump chain failure, turbo actuator faults and AdBlue system issues are the most common reasons UK Fiat Ducato owners need engine replacement. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing these known weaknesses. Every unit includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery options. Compare quotes today and get your commercial Ducato back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Fiat Ducato Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Fiat Ducato and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Fiat Ducato Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Fiat Ducato in the UK is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Fiat Ducato 2.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.3L MultiJet diesel is the backbone of the Fiat Ducato range, powering approximately 70% of X250 and X290 generation vans on UK roads. Introduced in 2006, it offers an optimal balance of fuel economy, torque and payload capacity for Panel Van, Chassis Cab and Motorhome applications, though EGR and DPF issues are common on short-journey commercial use.
commonFailurePoints: oil pump chain wear on early F1AE0481C units
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Ducato 2.3L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Ducato 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L MultiJet heavy-duty diesel serves Chassis Cab, Tipper, Dropside and high-GVW Motorhome applications requiring maximum torque and payload capacity. With reinforced internals and higher power output, it's the preferred choice for commercial converters and PSV operators, though turbo and AdBlue system faults are documented on high-mileage units.

items.title: Fiat Ducato 2.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.3L MultiJet diesel is the backbone of the Fiat Ducato range, powering approximately 70% of X250 and X290 generation vans on UK roads. Introduced in 2006, it offers an optimal balance of fuel economy, torque and payload capacity for Panel Van, Chassis Cab and Motorhome applications, though EGR and DPF issues are common on short-journey commercial use.

items.title: Fiat Ducato 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L MultiJet heavy-duty diesel serves Chassis Cab, Tipper, Dropside and high-GVW Motorhome applications requiring maximum torque and payload capacity. With reinforced internals and higher power output, it's the preferred choice for commercial converters and PSV operators, though turbo and AdBlue system faults are documented on high-mileage units.
commonFailurePoints: AdBlue SCR system faults on Euro 6 variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Ducato 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Ducato 2.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The legacy 2.8L SOFIM diesel powered early X250 Ducatos before the MultiJet transition. Mechanically simple with indirect injection, it's sought for classic Ducato restorations and budget replacements, though injection pump wear and turbo seal leaks are common on high-mileage examples.
compatibleModels: Early X250 Panel Van base spec (2006-2008)
Early X250 Chassis Cab entry level
commonFailurePoints: Injection pump wear causing hard starting
glow plug circuit failures in winter

Introduction of 2.3L F1AE MultiJet diesel alongside legacy 2.8L SOFIM
Early F1AE0481C engines feature oil pump chain drive with known failure risk
Panel Van L2H2/L3H2 and Chassis Cab 35 become core UK fleet configurations

faq.h2: Fiat Ducato Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Fiat Ducato engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Fiat Ducato engines typically cost £1,800-£2,400 for 2.3L MultiJet units and £2,200-£2,800 for 3.0L heavy-duty variants, supply only. Used engines range £1,200-£1,800 but carry higher risk; supply & fit adds £600-£1,200 depending on variant complexity and location.
keyPoints: 2.3L MultiJet rebuilt: £1,800-£2,400 supply only, most common for Panel Van
3.0L MultiJet rebuilt: £2,200-£2,800 supply only, for Chassis Cab/Tipper
Supply & fit service adds £600-£1,200 to engine price nationwide
Euro 6 AdBlue engines command premium due to ULEZ compliance demand
items.cta: Compare Fiat Ducato engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine code does my Fiat Ducato have and how do I check?
items.answer: Find your Fiat Ducato engine code on the VIN plate under the bonnet, in the service book, or by entering your registration on our free checker. Common codes are F1AE0481D, F1AE3481D (2.3L) and F1CE0481D, F1CE3481E (3.0L).
keyPoints: VIN plate under bonnet lists engine code starting F1AE or F1CE
Service book and MOT certificate often record engine code
Free reg lookup instantly identifies exact code and compatible replacements
Matching engine code ensures warranty validity and correct fitment
items.cta: Use our free Fiat Ducato engine code checker now

items.question: Is the Fiat Ducato 2.3L MultiJet engine reliable for commercial use?
items.answer: The Fiat Ducato 2.3L MultiJet is generally reliable for commercial use when properly maintained, but EGR valve clogging, DPF regeneration failure and turbo actuator faults are common on short-journey urban fleets. Early F1AE0481C units (pre-2010) carry oil pump chain failure risk.
keyPoints: EGR/DPF issues common on stop-start urban commercial duty cycles
Oil pump chain failure risk on early F1AE0481C engines before 2010
Regular motorway runs help DPF regeneration and extend component life
Rebuilt engines with upgraded EGR/chain components offer best long-term value
items.cta: Get quotes for upgraded Fiat Ducato 2.3L MultiJet rebuilt engines

items.question: Can I fit a Fiat Ducato replacement engine myself or do I need a specialist?
items.answer: While mechanically competent DIYers can fit a Fiat Ducato engine, commercial operators benefit from specialist supply & fit services that include warranty coverage, post-installation diagnostics and MoT emissions certification, minimising downtime risk.
keyPoints: Specialist fit ensures warranty validity and correct calibration of AdBlue/DPF systems
Supply & fit bundles reduce coordination overhead for fleet managers
Post-installation diagnostics verify emissions compliance for MoT testing
Nationwide network of Ducato-specialist garages available through EngineMarket
items.cta: Find Fiat Ducato engine supply & fit deals from vetted UK specialists

items.question: What warranty comes with a Fiat Ducato replacement engine for commercial use?
items.answer: Rebuilt Fiat Ducato engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty valid for commercial use, covering parts and labour at any UK garage; confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Warranty valid for commercial, fleet and motorhome converter applications
Covers parts and labour at any UK garage; no approved installer requirement
Always confirm warranty terms in writing before committing to purchase
items.cta: Compare Fiat Ducato engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: How long does Fiat Ducato engine replacement take and what's the downtime?
items.answer: Typical Fiat Ducato engine replacement takes 1-2 days for Panel Van variants and 2-3 days for Chassis Cab/Tipper with supply & fit; next-day engine dispatch available from most UK suppliers to minimise commercial downtime.
keyPoints: Next-day engine dispatch available from most UK specialist suppliers
Panel Van L2H2/L3H2 typically 1-2 day fitting timeline at specialist garage
Chassis Cab/Tipper variants may require 2-3 days due to complexity
Supply & fit coordination reduces total downtime vs separate supply/fit arrangements
items.cta: Get quotes for next-day Fiat Ducato engine delivery UK

items.question: Will a replacement Fiat Ducato engine pass MoT emissions testing?
items.answer: Yes, rebuilt Fiat Ducato engines supplied through EngineMarket are calibrated to meet original emissions specifications and include MoT emissions compliance certification; Euro 6 AdBlue units are ULEZ/CAZ compliant for urban commercial access.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines calibrated to original emissions specifications
MoT emissions compliance certification included with supply
Euro 6 AdBlue engines certified for ULEZ/CAZ urban access compliance
Post-installation diagnostics verify emissions performance before MoT test
items.cta: Compare Euro 6 Fiat Ducato engine prices with MoT compliance certification

items.question: Is a Fiat Ducato engine ULEZ compliant for London commercial use?
items.answer: Fiat Ducato engines with Euro 6 AdBlue calibration (F1AE3481E, F1CE3481E) are ULEZ compliant for London commercial use; Euro 4/5 engines incur daily charges. Rebuilt Euro 6 engines with AdBlue upgrade ensure ongoing compliance.
keyPoints: Euro 6 AdBlue engines (F1AE3481E/F1CE3481E) are ULEZ compliant
Euro 4/5 engines incur £12.50/day ULEZ charge for commercial vehicles
Rebuilt Euro 6 engines include AdBlue system upgrade for compliance
ULEZ compliance essential for London-based fleet and delivery operators
items.cta: Get quotes for ULEZ compliant Fiat Ducato Euro 6 engines

items.question: Can I upgrade my Fiat Ducato from 2.3L to 3.0L engine for more power?
items.answer: Upgrading a Fiat Ducato from 2.3L to 3.0L MultiJet is technically possible but requires significant modifications to engine mounts, cooling, exhaust and ECU calibration; for most commercial operators, a rebuilt 2.3L with upgraded components offers better value and simpler compliance.
keyPoints: 2.3L to 3.0L swap requires custom mounts, cooling and ECU recalibration
Rebuilt 2.3L with upgraded turbo/EGR often sufficient for commercial needs
3.0L preferred for Chassis Cab/Tipper requiring maximum payload capacity
Consult specialist before committing to ensure variant compatibility and compliance
items.cta: Enquire about Fiat Ducato heavy-duty 3.0L engine options

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt, reconditioned and used Fiat Ducato engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt Fiat Ducato engines undergo comprehensive overhaul with upgraded components addressing known failures; reconditioned units receive targeted repairs to worn parts; used engines are low-mileage donor units with variable history. Rebuilt offers best commercial value with warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: comprehensive overhaul, upgraded components, commercial warranty
Reconditioned: targeted repairs to worn parts, budget-friendly option
Used: low-mileage donor unit, verify history and compression before purchase
Rebuilt engines offer best long-term value for high-mileage commercial Ducatos
items.cta: Compare rebuilt, reconditioned and used Fiat Ducato engine prices
